name: Charlottetown
summary: Charlottetown is the small seaside capital of Prince Edward Island, Canada's tiniest province. It is famous as the birthplace of Canada, where leaders met in 1864, and for its bright red beaches and Anne of Green Gables stories.
funFacts: Canada was first dreamed up in Charlottetown in 1864, when leaders met at Province House to talk about joining their colonies into one big country.
PEI's beaches and country roads glow rust-red because the soil and sandstone are packed with iron, basically the same stuff that makes nails go rusty.
The Confederation Bridge that links PEI to the mainland is 12.9 kilometres long, the world's longest bridge built across waters that freeze every winter.
Anne of Green Gables, the famous redhead from PEI, was created by author Lucy Maud Montgomery and her book has been translated into more than 36 languages.
Prince Edward Island is so small you can drive across the whole province in just a few hours, yet it has its own cute potato-shaped licence plates.
thingsToDo: Tour Province House, the grand stone building where Canada's founding meeting happened in 1864.
Drive to Cavendish to visit Green Gables Heritage Place, the green-roofed farmhouse from the Anne stories.
Squish your toes in PEI's bright red sand at Cavendish or Brackley Beach in PEI National Park.
Walk Victoria Row, a car-free brick street with street musicians, ice cream shops and harbour views nearby.
Climb the spiral staircase of Point Prim Lighthouse, PEI's oldest and only round brick lighthouse.
Try fresh PEI lobster or a lobster roll at a waterfront restaurant on Peake's Quay boardwalk.

wildlife.name: Red Fox
wildlife.description: Bushy-tailed orange fox often seen trotting along PEI's red-dirt roads and farm fields.

wildlife.name: Harbour Seal
wildlife.description: Plump speckled seal that pops its round head out of PEI's bays and harbours.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Little black-and-white seabird with a bright rainbow-coloured beak nicknamed the sea parrot.

wildlife.name: Common Loon
wildlife.description: Black-and-white diving bird with red eyes and a haunting yodel call across PEI lakes.

localWord.meaning: Hello in Mi'kmaw, the language of PEI's first people, who call the island Epekwitk meaning 'cradled on the waves'.

images.alt: Boats and red-roofed buildings line the sunny Charlottetown waterfront on Prince Edward Island.
The grand stone Province House in Charlottetown, where Canada was first dreamed up.
The famous green-roofed Green Gables farmhouse from Anne of Green Gables, surrounded by flowers.
The long curving Confederation Bridge stretches across the sea to Prince Edward Island.
PEI's famous red sand beach at Blooming Point meets calm blue ocean water.
The tall round brick Point Prim Lighthouse glows at sunset on PEI's south coast.
A bright orange red fox with a bushy white-tipped tail on Prince Edward Island.
A speckled harbour seal resting with its round head raised.
Close-up of an Atlantic puffin's black-and-white face and bright rainbow beak.
A black-and-white common loon with red eyes swimming on a calm lake.
